creator: terry-freebsd@glaver.org
text:
(In reply to Doug Ambrisko from comment #7)
I'm not sure why it's called "query disk", though it probably was appropriate when this was originally added. Now it's adds to the confusion.

Also, why is mrsasutil going off into the weeds and saying "There is no Controller number X"? There is only one disk controller in this system, and that's mrsas0.

mftutil / mrsasutil both correctly output information on both logical volumes and physical drives. Refer to the new attachment mfiutil_mrsasutil.txt to see the output.

These two systems are similar (in that they have a mirrored pair of disks as the first volume) and then individual drives as volumes for the rest of the drives on the controller. The first one is from a controller that doesn't support mrsas. The second is from a controller that does support mrsas (and needs it enabled, as without setting the sysctl the system chooses the mfi driver, is slow and crashes).
